mission.title: Install and Configure Grafana Operator
mission.description: The Grafana Operator simplifies the deployment and management of Grafana instances and their resources in Kubernetes. It allows users to manage Grafana through custom resources, making it ideal for GitOps workflows. Key trade-offs include the need for Kubernetes knowledge and potential complexity in managing multiple instances.

steps.title: Add the Grafana Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the Grafana Helm chart repository to your Helm client, allowing you to install the Grafana Operator.

```bash

helm repo add grafana oci://ghcr.io/grafana/helm-charts

helm repo update

```

> Adding the Grafana Helm repository.
> Updating the Helm repository to get the latest charts.

steps.title: Install the Grafana Operator
steps.description:
This command installs the Grafana Operator using Helm, specifying the version to ensure compatibility.

```bash

helm upgrade -i grafana-operator oci://ghcr.io/grafana/helm-charts/grafana-operator --version 5.22.2

```

> Installing the Grafana Operator.

steps.title: Create a Grafana instance
steps.description:
This step creates a custom resource for a Grafana instance, configuring it with basic settings.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: grafana.integreatly.org/v1beta1
kind: Grafana
metadata:
  name: grafana
  labels:
    dashboards: "grafana"
spec:
  config:
    log:
      mode: "console"
    security:
      admin_user: root
      admin_password: <REDACTED>
EOF

```

> Creating a Grafana instance with basic configuration.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: grafana.integreatly.org/v1beta1
kind: Grafana
metadata:
  name: grafana
  labels:
    dashboards: "grafana"
spec:
  config:
    log:
      mode: "console"
    security:
      admin_user: root
      admin_password: <REDACTED>
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Grafana instance
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the Grafana instance to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get grafana grafana -o yaml

```

> Verifying the Grafana instance status.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the Grafana Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Grafana Operator and its associated resources. Be aware that this may lead to data loss for Grafana instances and dashboards managed by the operator.

```bash

helm uninstall grafana-operator

kubectl delete grafana grafana

```

> Uninstalling the Grafana Operator.
> Deleting the Grafana instance.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Grafana Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your Grafana configurations. This step will upgrade the Grafana Operator to the specified version.

```bash

kubectl get grafana grafana -o yaml > grafana-backup.yaml

helm upgrade grafana-operator oci://ghcr.io/grafana/helm-charts/grafana-operator --version 5.22.2

kubectl get grafana grafana -o yaml

```

> Backing up the current Grafana configuration.
> Upgrading the Grafana Operator.
> Verifying the Grafana instance status post-upgrade.
commands: kubectl get grafana grafana -o yaml > grafana-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Grafana instance not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Grafana instance may not have been created successfully.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the Grafana Operator for errors: kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=grafana-operator

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to install Grafana'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Helm chart version may not be compatible with the current Kubernetes version.

**Fix:**
Ensure you are using a compatible Helm chart version with your Kubernetes version.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unauthorized access to Grafana'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The admin credentials may not be set correctly in the Grafana custom resource.

**Fix:**
Verify the admin credentials in the Grafana custom resource and update if necessary.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Grafana dashboard not rendering'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Grafana dashboard configuration may be incorrect or missing.

**Fix:**
Check the GrafanaDashboard custom resource for correct JSON configuration.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new Grafana features and improved CRD validation.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the Grafana instance running with the command: kubectl get grafana grafana -o yaml, showing the status as 'Running'.

spec:

    log:
      mode: "console"
    security:
      admin_user: root
      admin_password: <REDACTED>
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the required tools installed.
